event.narrative_summary: US-Iran tensions are escalating with Trump threatening to hit Iran "extremely hard" and reports of additional US troops deploying to the Middle East. Gold initially sold off 1.8% on the troop news despite being the classic geopolitical hedge, suggesting the move is being driven by margin calls and liquidity needs rather than fundamentals. Crypto also sold off in tandem, confirming a broad risk-off deleveraging event across alternative assets.
event.trading_play:
BULL_PCT: 40
BULL: If tensions de-escalate through diplomatic channels or Iran signals compliance on oil/nuclear terms, then risk assets rebound sharply as the fear premium unwinds. Consider long GLD calls 2-4 weeks out, as gold's counterintuitive drop creates a dislocation — geopolitical gold bids historically reassert within days once forced selling clears.

BEAR_PCT: 60
BEAR: If military action materializes or Iran retaliates against oil infrastructure, then energy spikes trigger broader equity drawdowns and sustained safe-haven flows into USD/treasuries over gold. Consider short USO puts or long UUP 1-2 week expiry, as dollar strength and oil volatility become the dominant trades in an active conflict scenario.
